headline: D94b rerun result was corrupted by a Claude usage-limit message — corrected
message: The rerun that reported 95.8%/12.5% success was invalid: claude -p hit this account's weekly usage limit mid-run and printed "You've hit your weekly limit" to stdout, which the harness accepted as a literal student answer instead of erroring. 26 of the 26 combined "successful" jobs across both models were this same placeholder text, not real model output (the grader correctly scored it at the floor, which is why every axis showed exactly 1.0 — that was the grader working correctly on garbage input). Both models have been reset to their true state: 0/24. The other 14 models in this dataset were checked and are unaffected.
notes: Root-cause diagnoses from the original D94 investigation still stand: llama-3.3-70b-instruct is NVIDIA-side worker-queue overload (503, confirmed via raw HTTP diagnostic), nemotron-super-49b-v1.5 needs a larger max_tokens as a reasoning model (confirmed via raw HTTP diagnostic).
The D94b rerun attempt itself failed for an unrelated reason: this account's Claude weekly usage limit was exhausted mid-run.
Weekly limit resets 2026-07-11 12:00 (Asia/Seoul) — retry blocked until then.
Correction applied by fix_quota_contaminated_results.py, which flags contaminated rows explicitly rather than silently deleting them.

meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct.top_error_summary: 21x HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout, 3x contaminated (weekly-limit message misread as answer)

nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5.top_error_summary: 23x contaminated (weekly-limit message misread as answer), 1x HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
